quiz.title: Unit 8 Quiz 10, Biology I
quiz.subtitle: Immunology: Innate and Adaptive Immunity
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: First Line of Defense, Innate vs Adaptive Immunity, Phagocytes, Inflammation, Complement Proteins, Natural Killer Cells, Lysozyme, Fever, B Cells, T Cells, Antibodies

questions.question: The first line of defense in the immune system includes:
questions.explanation: Mucous membranes are part of the first line of defense (physical and chemical barriers). Antibodies, T cells, and B cells are part of the adaptive immune system (third line of defense).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT part of innate immunity?
questions.explanation: Antibody production is part of adaptive immunity, not innate immunity. Phagocytosis, inflammation, and natural killer cells are all components of the innate immune system.

questions.question: Phagocytes include:
questions.explanation: Neutrophils and macrophages are phagocytes that engulf and destroy pathogens. B and T cells are lymphocytes (adaptive immunity), red blood cells carry oxygen, and platelets are for clotting.

questions.question: Select all that apply: Inflammation helps by:
questions.explanation: Inflammation increases blood flow (bringing immune cells) and recruits immune cells to the infection site. Antibodies are produced by B cells (adaptive immunity), inflammation does not destroy DNA or suppress immune response.

questions.question: Select all that apply: Complement proteins:
questions.explanation: Complement proteins enhance phagocytosis (opsonization), lyse pathogens (membrane attack complex), and are normally inactive until activated by infection. They are produced by the liver, not B cells, and are part of innate immunity.

questions.question: Select all that apply: Natural killer (NK) cells primarily target:
questions.explanation: Natural killer cells target virus-infected cells and cancerous cells. They do not target red blood cells, gut bacteria (that's phagocytes), or platelets.

questions.question: Select all that apply: Lysozyme in tears and saliva functions to:
questions.explanation: Lysozyme is an enzyme that breaks down bacterial cell walls by cleaving peptidoglycan. It does not digest proteins generally, stimulate T cells, or produce antibodies.

questions.question: Fever is an innate immune response that:
questions.explanation: Fever inhibits pathogen growth by raising body temperature, enhances immune cell activity, and speeds up repair. It does not directly kill all pathogens, reduce inflammation, or activate B cells directly.

questions.question: The innate immune system responds:
questions.explanation: Innate immunity responds rapidly (minutes to hours) as the first line of defense. Adaptive immunity takes days to weeks to respond but provides specific long-term protection.

questions.question: Skin produces antimicrobial oils and sweat as part of:
questions.explanation: Skin's antimicrobial secretions are part of innate immunity (chemical barriers). Adaptive immunity includes humoral (antibodies) and cell-mediated (T cells) responses.

questions.question: Adaptive immunity is characterized by:
questions.explanation: Adaptive immunity is characterized by specificity (targets specific pathogens) and memory (remembers past infections). Innate immunity is non-specific and immediate.

questions.question: B cells produce:
questions.explanation: B cells produce antibodies (immunoglobulins) as part of humoral immunity. Cytokines are produced by various immune cells, phagocytes are innate cells, and NK cells are innate lymphocytes.

questions.question: T cells mature in:
questions.explanation: T cells mature in the thymus (T for thymus). B cells mature in bone marrow (B for bone marrow). Spleen and lymph nodes are secondary lymphoid organs where immune responses occur.

questions.question: Helper T cells (CD4+) function to:
questions.explanation: Helper T cells activate B cells, cytotoxic T cells, and macrophages. Cytotoxic T cells kill infected cells, B cells produce antibodies, and phagocytes directly destroy pathogens.

questions.question: Antibodies are also called:
questions.explanation: Antibodies are also known as immunoglobulins (Ig). Cytokines are signaling molecules, complement proteins are innate immune proteins, and interferons are antiviral cytokines.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the immune system and its components! 🛡️
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the differences between innate and adaptive immunity.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on immune cell functions and inflammation.
scoreMessages.0: Please review first line of defense, phagocytes, and antibody production.
